item.title: 2025超詳細ipad平板保護殼選購指南！折疊？磁吸？該咋選！差價20倍，差別有多大？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:title: 2025超詳細ipad平板保護殼選購指南！折疊？磁吸？該咋選！差價20倍，差別有多大？｜大狸子切切裡
item.itunes:author: 大狸子切切裡 Cherry OFFICIAL CHANNEL
item.itunes:summary:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qF3Bd3VnbnE

值得閱讀的理由

  • 了解如何挑選適合自己的平板保護殼。
  • 認識不同保護殼類型的優缺點，尤其在支架、重量和材質方面。
  • 避免踩到購買平板保護殼的常見陷阱，省下冤枉錢。

摘要

開場：平板保護殼的兩難

作者開頭表達對於平板保護殼的複雜感受，認為它們不像手機殼單純，需要同時具備保護、支撐、增加功能甚至支援打字等多重用途，同時又不能太重，導致沒有完美方案。他提到無論價格高低，市面上許多保護殼令人失望。因此，他決定透過這部影片，幫助觀眾釐清支架、重量、材質與細節的重要性，避免重蹈他的覆轍。他強調這支影片絕非業配。

支架的重要性與基本類型

作者認為平板保護殼的支架功能並非選配，而是必需品。他用「沒有蓋子的泡麵」來比喻沒有支架的平板。不同的支架類型適合不同需求。他指出向後翻摺的保護殼通常實用、結構緊湊簡單，並支援低角度模式，特別適合繪畫或書寫，能改善眩光問題。他甚至認為一些第三方保護殼比官方的更好，例如會增加筆槽。作者也批評Apple昂貴的Smart Folio。

基本保護殼的選擇與陷阱

作者比較了兩款外觀幾乎相同的基本保護殼（價格分別低於10美元和約20美元），指出即使價格不同，外觀可能一樣。他特別警告喜歡用餐時使用平板的人，不要購買這種保護殼，因為其外部材質容易吸附桌面油污並沾染螢幕。此外，這類保護殼通常無法調整角度，觀看影片不便。對此，作者推薦向內翻摺的保護殼，這類保護殼較抗污且角度調整更靈活。或者，選擇結構簡單的「真支架」保護殼。

多功能與複雜支架設計

作者接著介紹了近期流行的「摺紙」式保護殼，這類保護殼宣稱功能比官方更多樣。他展示了一款能提供多種角度（低角度、直立/橫向）的保護殼，並實際示範了複雜的摺疊方式，指出某些模式相當繁瑣。他也展示了另一款帶磁吸背板的摺紙殼，它甚至可以分離並支援直立模式，作者雖然覺得「神奇」，但也表示越複雜的摺紙殼，展開後就越顯雜亂。作者提到團隊測試時都被其複雜性搞得一愣。作者也提到了市面上的旋轉式保護殼，但他並不喜歡，因為這類保護殼如果操作不當，容易發出刺耳的聲響。他建議如果真的需要直立模式，不如選擇磁吸式保護殼。

重量與保護性的權衡

作者接著探討保護殼的重量與保護性之間的權衡。保護性越差、結構越少，通常越輕。最輕薄的選項類似Apple官方的磁吸式保護蓋，具有簡單的兩段式支架，但邊緣暴露，保護性較差。全包覆式保護殼提供更好的保護，但會顯著增加重量（通常200-300克，帶加固背板的甚至超過400克）。作者觀察到，昂貴的保護殼往往笨重或設計不佳，似乎是為了堆砌規格而犧牲了使用體驗。若追求輕薄，建議選擇中階保護殼或Apple官方產品。

材質選擇與問題

在材質方面，作者表示平板保護殼的變化不如手機殼多。他提到了常見的幾種類型，以及一些較不傳統的材質。質感上，他認為親膚材質和人造皮革較佳，但親膚材質非常不耐髒，容易沾染油污，且邊緣可能出現剝落。

平價鍵盤保護殼的實測與批判

作者接著評測了市面上最重、功能最複雜的一類保護殼：藍牙鍵盤保護殼，這些產品常被定位為Apple Magic Keyboard（售價約330美元）的平價替代品。他指出這些幾十美元的鍵盤殼外觀確實相似，也支援自動配對和無線充電（指鍵盤本身）。然而，他強調Magic Keyboard的核心價值在於其整合的充電孔和能無縫切換到平板模式的順暢體驗。對於這些平價鍵盤殼，作者給予嚴厲批判。它們需要獨立充電（保留Type-C孔），展開時顯得雜亂。最關鍵的是，滑鼠存在明顯的延遲，導致難以精準點擊，體驗非常糟糕（比喻為「像開車一樣」需要提前煞車）。即使從支架上取下或闔上蓋子，藍牙仍會保持連接，導致螢幕鍵盤無法彈出。其中一款的觸控板中心區域難以按壓。此外，這些鍵盤殼非常笨重（超過600克，比iPad Pro本身還重）。作者認為以這種糟糕的使用體驗來說，幾十美元的價格仍然太高。他建議，如果真的需要鍵盤，不如選擇真正的第三方鍵盤搭配單獨的支架，這比這些「半成品」模仿品要好得多。

各種奇葩保護殼大賞

作者接著展示了一些他平常絕不會購買的「奇葩保護殼」。首先是一款將iPad變成「兒童平板」的保護殼，其筆槽設計奇特。接著是一款帶有把手的保護殼，把手感覺很不牢固，且沒有正規支架，只能靠不穩定的方式站立。還有一款「氣囊保護殼」，作者稱其背部的吸震能力一流，設計有模仿縫線的氣囊紋路。另一款外觀類似少女剪貼簿風格的保護殼，帶有麂皮質感和底部網格，作者認為網格部分廉價鬆垮，但設計可愛，售價19美元，意外地實用，甚至可以擦拭螢幕。一款設計類似Mac Studio的磁吸保護殼，售價52美元，但作者拿到時磁鐵脫落，使用時會發出令人不適的金屬刮擦聲。儘管外觀抽象，作者發現它意外地堅固，難以彎曲，內部還有一個不知用途的芳香片。一款模組化的「散熱保護殼」，售價68美元，背面有類似MagSafe的接口，可以外接散熱器。作者質疑iPad Pro是否真的需要主動散熱，並認為這款保護殼容易丟失零件，不便攜帶。最後是一款僅售幾美元的超薄保護殼，作者認為其做工差、中間大塊鏤空，實際裝上後非常薄，保護性令人存疑。

================================================

All along,. I've been really conflicted about tablet cases. For phones， no matter how fancy you make them,. it's all about materials and small details. At most, you add a leather case or a finger ring, right? But for tablets,. they expect the case. to protect,. to stand,. to add all kinds of mechanisms,. and ideally,. even to support typing. Oh, and. don't make it too heavy. So you can guess that. there's just no perfect solution. So,. these are the cases I bought this time. Oh my god, it's 2025 already,. but some cases still make me want to rip them off instantly. A 10-dollar case and a 20-dollar case. look exactly the same. And some cases that cost over 50 dollars. don't work well at all. So,. let me help you to figure out. how important the stand, weight, material. and some little details are. After this, you'll know exactly what to buy without regrets. Don't step into the same traps I did. This video is absolutely not sponsored. Let's get started. A tablet without a stand. is like instant noodles without a lid. I think a stand isn't optional. It's a must. But stands. aren't all the same. You need to pick based on your needs. Cases that fold backward like this. are usually useful. They have compact and simple structure,. and support low-angle mode. Especially if you draw or write,. hey,. laying it flat causes glare and color shifts,. and propping it up slightly fixes that. But honestly, some third-party cases. are better than the official ones. Same with magnetic attachment,. they also add a clasp. to secure the stylus. More importantly,. Apple's Smart Folio. costs about 80 dollars.

Might as well rob me. "Smart", my foot! But these third-party ones are also crazy. These two cases, for example. One costs under 10 dollars. and the other about 20 dollars. Can you spot any difference? Sounds like they're full of merits,. but for those who like to take their tablets out for meals,. heed my warning:. don't buy this kind of case. The outside is basically a rag. that soaks up all the grease from the table. and smears it on your screen. Plus, these cases usually don't adjust angles. and you have to move your head. So if you watch a lot of videos, get an inward-folding case. It's stain-resistant and more adjustable. This type of case. is also slightly sturdier. Or go for a real stand case,. but keep the design simple. Lately, a new type of folding case has become popular. It can be folded like origami. Look at this seller's show. It always has more functions than the official case. For example, this one. There are two low angles. And it raises for portrait or landscape mode. Let me show you how it works. As for the first mode,. fold it inward like this,. tuck it here,. and that's angle one. Flip it around. and it's angle two. The third mode. is quite tricky. First push outward,. and then it stands up. If you reverse this part,. that's another angle. But that's not the official way. Here is another one with a magnetic backplate. It's even crazier. First, lay it flat like this,. then flip it outward,. and it stands like this for use. After raising it,. it can actually detach. Then you can switch the angle. for portrait mode. Just amazing. Ignoring other factors,. I'd totally pick this one. But the more complex the origami case,. the messier it looks when unfolded.

Let me put it this way:. when I tested it with my team,. everyone froze for a second. There's also a rotating case on the market,. but I'm not a fan. Though it unfolds easily,. if not handled properly,. it creaks like crazy. If you really need portrait mode,. just get a magnetic case instead. But usually, different types. mean different weights. The worse the protection. and the fewer the structures,. the lighter it is. So the thinnest option. is the type like Apple's official magnetic folio. Tri-fold with two angles. It works for watching videos and writing. It's both a cover. and a stand. Exactly a simple work of art. But with exposed edges,. 'm still worried. Some of my teammates even said. they added edge film to use magnetic cases safely. Full-coverage cases offer better protection. but add weight,. usually 200 to 300g. You can try holding a box of milk with your iPad. to feel the weight. If it is the type with a reinforced backplate,. it would have to reach over 400 grams. It is equivalent to an 11-inch iPad Pro. And this type of case is super expensive. Unless you're really anxious about damage,. the experiebce of this kind is not the best. What's weird is that. pricey cases are usually bulky. or ugly,. like they're over-engineered for specs. to force "premium" vibes. So if you want a thin and light one, just choose the mid-range cases,. or Apple's own. As for materials,. tablet cases. aren't as flashy as phone cases. They are basically these types. Other materials also exist,. like cowhide case, portable protective case for children,. and velvet-mesh case. People with high EQ would say. it's a bit unconventional. For the texture,. soft-touch and faux leather win. But for stain resistance,.

soft-touch is awful. as it's easy to get smeared with grease. If it's pressed like this,. the edges might peel later. Anyone else's case peeling? Raise your hand and let me see. Take a look at these two. which have the most complex functions. and are the heaviest. The Magic Keyboard,. but. Bluetooth edition. But this is what I said. They'd never admit it. Everyone knows. Apple's Magic Keyboard is made of gold,. costing just about 330 dollars. All these cases claim to be its budget alternatives. They support auto-pairing,. even wireless and something else. And they are only around 40 dollars. So gerenous. But really? Well, yes. They can connect automatically. After all, it's Bluetooth. And they look similar to the Apple's, indeed. But the essence of the Magic Keyboard. is not just the keyboard. This port. can charge your iPad. And when you take it off,. it seamlessly transforms into tablet mode for continued use. The smooth feel. when opening and closing it. just makes perfect sense. Now, look at these others. they keep a Type-C port because the keyboard itself needs charging,. and when you open them up, it's just a mess. They're like new recruits trying to do yoga. without stretching first. Well, let's step back and say,. even if we just use it as a Bluetooth keyboard,. isn't it still decent? emmmmmmmmmmm. If you can tolerate the mouse delay, that is. iPad OS already has this exaggerated mouse acceleration,. and with the added delay,. you can never click exactly where you want. You have to brake in advance, just like driving a car. Even if you take it off the stand,. it's still connected. No matter how many times you click, the keyboard won't pop up. It's still connected even when you close the lid. And here's the kicker. one of them has a trackpad.

that supports full-area press,. but while it works around the edges,. pressing in the middle. is a struggle. After using it for a few minutes, I felt very frustrated. Is that it? Nope. Both keyboards weigh over 600 grams,. much heavier than the iPad Pro itself. At this point,. I suddenly thought,. why is the 40-plus-dollar price tag so steep? My advice is that. if you really need a keyboard,. you're better off finding a genuine third-party keyboard,. one with a stand. and a chin support. Those are way better than these half-baked copies. that are even worse than generic models. Now, taking this opportunity,. let's talk about cases I'd never normally consider buying. Ta-da! Here's a basket full. Once you slap one of these on,. your iPad turns into a kid's tablet. And here's how you. put the pen in. you have to poke it in horizontally like this,. then slide it back. Perfect for preventing kids from knocking it out. But seriously, which genius. came up with the idea of making a child-friendly case for the iPad Pro? Now, take a look at this one. This is even more outrageous. Look at this handle. it feels like it's going to snap off in my hand any second. And there's no stand either. You place it here,. and sure, it stands up,. but it feels like a gentle push would topple it over. Next up is this airbag case. The back of this thing is definitely top-notch for shock absorption. They've actually added these dashed lines here,. giving it a vibe like the airbags are stitched on. Let me show you another case. actually, it looks pretty cute,. kind of like a teenage girl's scrapbook style. It's got this slightly suede-like finish,. and there's a mesh layer at the bottom. But the mesh isn't taut at all; it looks kind of cheap and flimsy. And don't let its looks fool you. it's not cheap, either. It costs $19. Though I have to say, it's kinda practical. you can even use it to wipe your screen! Now, onto the next.

iPad case... Wait,. from Mac Studio. it's priced at $52,. but look. the magnet has already flown off! Time for some on-the-spot repairs. Don't. oh wow, that sound! Is there anyone out there. who can't stand the sound of metal scraping? Steer clear of this one, then. The scraping sound. is terrifying. Actually, despite its abstract design,. this thing is way sturdier than I expected. Let me show you how tough it is. you can't even bend it! And get this, there's even an aromatherapy tablet inside. But I'm not sure. what the point of that is. And then there's this weird cooling case. so modular,. and it costs $68. There's a MagSafe port at the back,. so you can attach a cooler for active cooling. But does the iPad Pro really need active cooling? Plus, this case is a nightmare. for carrying around—it's so easy to lose parts! Now, let's talk about this iPad case that costs just over a few bucks. Wow,. the craftsmanship is truly... uninspiring, to say the least. There's this huge hollowed-out section in the middle. I doubt. it uses much more material than a phone case. And as expected, when I put it on,. it's so thin that I seriously doubt it offers any real protection. By the way, when I was filming this video,. this case also arrived. The texture is truly... unbeatable. This super cool-looking case. costs $36. When I bought it,. the description said,. "Handmade, top-grain leather, genuine leather. ￥@#￥………… #@￥#￥. case." Honestly, it looks decent from the outside,. but the inside is kinda grimy. It feels like they just bought a generic case mold from somewhere. and slapped this leather on top. I'd say even $1 is too much. for that generic case part. I feel like I could tear it off. with a little effort.

The texture is just... unbeatable, as I said. So,. which one do you think offers better value? Actually, iPad cases these days are pretty decent overall. You generally get what you pay for,. and there aren't too many outrageous rip-offs. But there are a few special features you need to watch out for,. like the Apple Pencil slot. Some cases have this gap here,. and since the magnetic hold isn't super strong to begin with,. once you take it. out of your bag,. the pencil's basically guaranteed to fall out. At the very least, there should be a flap or a groove. to keep the pencil secure. Another thing is the charging port,. especially with thicker cases. You either need a larger opening. or a thinner cable. Trying to force a thick cable into a small opening. is just asking for trouble. Also, I'd recommend avoiding plastic soft cases. Tablets are often placed on various surfaces,. and these cases get oily easily. If they turn yellow over time,. it's like a triple whammy. of disappointment. Here's another interesting thing. you'll notice. there are several cases out there. that look almost identical. but have different prices. and slightly different promotional images. But chances are,. once you get them in hand, they're pretty much the same,. or even exactly the same. So, if you see similar cases,. just go for the cheaper one,. regardless of the brand. When it comes to choosing cases for Android tablets,. since their sales volume isn't as large. or consistent as iPads,. my advice is. to first check out the official cases to see if any catch your eye. If you like one, go for the official option. If not,. I've got a somewhat unconventional tip. search for iPad cases instead,. find a style you like. since those have usually been tested over time,. and then look for. a similar style for your Android tablet. Combine that with the tips I mentioned earlier about avoiding common pitfalls,. and you'll significantly reduce the chances of ending up with a dud. OK, so that's all for this video.

If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me. I'm Cherry. See you next time! Bye-bye.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qF3Bd3VnbnE

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

平板殼的花樣真的太多啦！

往期影片：

https://youtu.be/TnWxQ_6uo-Y

https://youtu.be/2rrFX_xR5BE

https://youtu.be/x8TaN2Mr4Nw

F O L L O W M E

Weibo: @大狸子切切里

Bilibili: https://space.bilibili.com/471303350

#數碼 #開箱 #保護殼 #平板 #ipad
item.description:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qF3Bd3VnbnE

值得閱讀的理由

  • 了解如何挑選適合自己的平板保護殼。
  • 認識不同保護殼類型的優缺點，尤其在支架、重量和材質方面。
  • 避免踩到購買平板保護殼的常見陷阱，省下冤枉錢。

摘要

開場：平板保護殼的兩難

作者開頭表達對於平板保護殼的複雜感受，認為它們不像手機殼單純，需要同時具備保護、支撐、增加功能甚至支援打字等多重用途，同時又不能太重，導致沒有完美方案。他提到無論價格高低，市面上許多保護殼令人失望。因此，他決定透過這部影片，幫助觀眾釐清支架、重量、材質與細節的重要性，避免重蹈他的覆轍。他強調這支影片絕非業配。

支架的重要性與基本類型

作者認為平板保護殼的支架功能並非選配，而是必需品。他用「沒有蓋子的泡麵」來比喻沒有支架的平板。不同的支架類型適合不同需求。他指出向後翻摺的保護殼通常實用、結構緊湊簡單，並支援低角度模式，特別適合繪畫或書寫，能改善眩光問題。他甚至認為一些第三方保護殼比官方的更好，例如會增加筆槽。作者也批評Apple昂貴的Smart Folio。

基本保護殼的選擇與陷阱

作者比較了兩款外觀幾乎相同的基本保護殼（價格分別低於10美元和約20美元），指出即使價格不同，外觀可能一樣。他特別警告喜歡用餐時使用平板的人，不要購買這種保護殼，因為其外部材質容易吸附桌面油污並沾染螢幕。此外，這類保護殼通常無法調整角度，觀看影片不便。對此，作者推薦向內翻摺的保護殼，這類保護殼較抗污且角度調整更靈活。或者，選擇結構簡單的「真支架」保護殼。

多功能與複雜支架設計

作者接著介紹了近期流行的「摺紙」式保護殼，這類保護殼宣稱功能比官方更多樣。他展示了一款能提供多種角度（低角度、直立/橫向）的保護殼，並實際示範了複雜的摺疊方式，指出某些模式相當繁瑣。他也展示了另一款帶磁吸背板的摺紙殼，它甚至可以分離並支援直立模式，作者雖然覺得「神奇」，但也表示越複雜的摺紙殼，展開後就越顯雜亂。作者提到團隊測試時都被其複雜性搞得一愣。作者也提到了市面上的旋轉式保護殼，但他並不喜歡，因為這類保護殼如果操作不當，容易發出刺耳的聲響。他建議如果真的需要直立模式，不如選擇磁吸式保護殼。

重量與保護性的權衡

作者接著探討保護殼的重量與保護性之間的權衡。保護性越差、結構越少，通常越輕。最輕薄的選項類似Apple官方的磁吸式保護蓋，具有簡單的兩段式支架，但邊緣暴露，保護性較差。全包覆式保護殼提供更好的保護，但會顯著增加重量（通常200-300克，帶加固背板的甚至超過400克）。作者觀察到，昂貴的保護殼往往笨重或設計不佳，似乎是為了堆砌規格而犧牲了使用體驗。若追求輕薄，建議選擇中階保護殼或Apple官方產品。

材質選擇與問題

在材質方面，作者表示平板保護殼的變化不如手機殼多。他提到了常見的幾種類型，以及一些較不傳統的材質。質感上，他認為親膚材質和人造皮革較佳，但親膚材質非常不耐髒，容易沾染油污，且邊緣可能出現剝落。

平價鍵盤保護殼的實測與批判

作者接著評測了市面上最重、功能最複雜的一類保護殼：藍牙鍵盤保護殼，這些產品常被定位為Apple Magic Keyboard（售價約330美元）的平價替代品。他指出這些幾十美元的鍵盤殼外觀確實相似，也支援自動配對和無線充電（指鍵盤本身）。然而，他強調Magic Keyboard的核心價值在於其整合的充電孔和能無縫切換到平板模式的順暢體驗。對於這些平價鍵盤殼，作者給予嚴厲批判。它們需要獨立充電（保留Type-C孔），展開時顯得雜亂。最關鍵的是，滑鼠存在明顯的延遲，導致難以精準點擊，體驗非常糟糕（比喻為「像開車一樣」需要提前煞車）。即使從支架上取下或闔上蓋子，藍牙仍會保持連接，導致螢幕鍵盤無法彈出。其中一款的觸控板中心區域難以按壓。此外，這些鍵盤殼非常笨重（超過600克，比iPad Pro本身還重）。作者認為以這種糟糕的使用體驗來說，幾十美元的價格仍然太高。他建議，如果真的需要鍵盤，不如選擇真正的第三方鍵盤搭配單獨的支架，這比這些「半成品」模仿品要好得多。

各種奇葩保護殼大賞

作者接著展示了一些他平常絕不會購買的「奇葩保護殼」。首先是一款將iPad變成「兒童平板」的保護殼，其筆槽設計奇特。接著是一款帶有把手的保護殼，把手感覺很不牢固，且沒有正規支架，只能靠不穩定的方式站立。還有一款「氣囊保護殼」，作者稱其背部的吸震能力一流，設計有模仿縫線的氣囊紋路。另一款外觀類似少女剪貼簿風格的保護殼，帶有麂皮質感和底部網格，作者認為網格部分廉價鬆垮，但設計可愛，售價19美元，意外地實用，甚至可以擦拭螢幕。一款設計類似Mac Studio的磁吸保護殼，售價52美元，但作者拿到時磁鐵脫落，使用時會發出令人不適的金屬刮擦聲。儘管外觀抽象，作者發現它意外地堅固，難以彎曲，內部還有一個不知用途的芳香片。一款模組化的「散熱保護殼」，售價68美元，背面有類似MagSafe的接口，可以外接散熱器。作者質疑iPad Pro是否真的需要主動散熱，並認為這款保護殼容易丟失零件，不便攜帶。最後是一款僅售幾美元的超薄保護殼，作者認為其做工差、中間大塊鏤空，實際裝上後非常薄，保護性令人存疑。

================================================

All along,. I've been really conflicted about tablet cases. For phones， no matter how fancy you make them,. it's all about materials and small details. At most, you add a leather case or a finger ring, right? But for tablets,. they expect the case. to protect,. to stand,. to add all kinds of mechanisms,. and ideally,. even to support typing. Oh, and. don't make it too heavy. So you can guess that. there's just no perfect solution. So,. these are the cases I bought this time. Oh my god, it's 2025 already,. but some cases still make me want to rip them off instantly. A 10-dollar case and a 20-dollar case. look exactly the same. And some cases that cost over 50 dollars. don't work well at all. So,. let me help you to figure out. how important the stand, weight, material. and some little details are. After this, you'll know exactly what to buy without regrets. Don't step into the same traps I did. This video is absolutely not sponsored. Let's get started. A tablet without a stand. is like instant noodles without a lid. I think a stand isn't optional. It's a must. But stands. aren't all the same. You need to pick based on your needs. Cases that fold backward like this. are usually useful. They have compact and simple structure,. and support low-angle mode. Especially if you draw or write,. hey,. laying it flat causes glare and color shifts,. and propping it up slightly fixes that. But honestly, some third-party cases. are better than the official ones. Same with magnetic attachment,. they also add a clasp. to secure the stylus. More importantly,. Apple's Smart Folio. costs about 80 dollars.

Might as well rob me. "Smart", my foot! But these third-party ones are also crazy. These two cases, for example. One costs under 10 dollars. and the other about 20 dollars. Can you spot any difference? Sounds like they're full of merits,. but for those who like to take their tablets out for meals,. heed my warning:. don't buy this kind of case. The outside is basically a rag. that soaks up all the grease from the table. and smears it on your screen. Plus, these cases usually don't adjust angles. and you have to move your head. So if you watch a lot of videos, get an inward-folding case. It's stain-resistant and more adjustable. This type of case. is also slightly sturdier. Or go for a real stand case,. but keep the design simple. Lately, a new type of folding case has become popular. It can be folded like origami. Look at this seller's show. It always has more functions than the official case. For example, this one. There are two low angles. And it raises for portrait or landscape mode. Let me show you how it works. As for the first mode,. fold it inward like this,. tuck it here,. and that's angle one. Flip it around. and it's angle two. The third mode. is quite tricky. First push outward,. and then it stands up. If you reverse this part,. that's another angle. But that's not the official way. Here is another one with a magnetic backplate. It's even crazier. First, lay it flat like this,. then flip it outward,. and it stands like this for use. After raising it,. it can actually detach. Then you can switch the angle. for portrait mode. Just amazing. Ignoring other factors,. I'd totally pick this one. But the more complex the origami case,. the messier it looks when unfolded.

Let me put it this way:. when I tested it with my team,. everyone froze for a second. There's also a rotating case on the market,. but I'm not a fan. Though it unfolds easily,. if not handled properly,. it creaks like crazy. If you really need portrait mode,. just get a magnetic case instead. But usually, different types. mean different weights. The worse the protection. and the fewer the structures,. the lighter it is. So the thinnest option. is the type like Apple's official magnetic folio. Tri-fold with two angles. It works for watching videos and writing. It's both a cover. and a stand. Exactly a simple work of art. But with exposed edges,. 'm still worried. Some of my teammates even said. they added edge film to use magnetic cases safely. Full-coverage cases offer better protection. but add weight,. usually 200 to 300g. You can try holding a box of milk with your iPad. to feel the weight. If it is the type with a reinforced backplate,. it would have to reach over 400 grams. It is equivalent to an 11-inch iPad Pro. And this type of case is super expensive. Unless you're really anxious about damage,. the experiebce of this kind is not the best. What's weird is that. pricey cases are usually bulky. or ugly,. like they're over-engineered for specs. to force "premium" vibes. So if you want a thin and light one, just choose the mid-range cases,. or Apple's own. As for materials,. tablet cases. aren't as flashy as phone cases. They are basically these types. Other materials also exist,. like cowhide case, portable protective case for children,. and velvet-mesh case. People with high EQ would say. it's a bit unconventional. For the texture,. soft-touch and faux leather win. But for stain resistance,.

soft-touch is awful. as it's easy to get smeared with grease. If it's pressed like this,. the edges might peel later. Anyone else's case peeling? Raise your hand and let me see. Take a look at these two. which have the most complex functions. and are the heaviest. The Magic Keyboard,. but. Bluetooth edition. But this is what I said. They'd never admit it. Everyone knows. Apple's Magic Keyboard is made of gold,. costing just about 330 dollars. All these cases claim to be its budget alternatives. They support auto-pairing,. even wireless and something else. And they are only around 40 dollars. So gerenous. But really? Well, yes. They can connect automatically. After all, it's Bluetooth. And they look similar to the Apple's, indeed. But the essence of the Magic Keyboard. is not just the keyboard. This port. can charge your iPad. And when you take it off,. it seamlessly transforms into tablet mode for continued use. The smooth feel. when opening and closing it. just makes perfect sense. Now, look at these others. they keep a Type-C port because the keyboard itself needs charging,. and when you open them up, it's just a mess. They're like new recruits trying to do yoga. without stretching first. Well, let's step back and say,. even if we just use it as a Bluetooth keyboard,. isn't it still decent? emmmmmmmmmmm. If you can tolerate the mouse delay, that is. iPad OS already has this exaggerated mouse acceleration,. and with the added delay,. you can never click exactly where you want. You have to brake in advance, just like driving a car. Even if you take it off the stand,. it's still connected. No matter how many times you click, the keyboard won't pop up. It's still connected even when you close the lid. And here's the kicker. one of them has a trackpad.

that supports full-area press,. but while it works around the edges,. pressing in the middle. is a struggle. After using it for a few minutes, I felt very frustrated. Is that it? Nope. Both keyboards weigh over 600 grams,. much heavier than the iPad Pro itself. At this point,. I suddenly thought,. why is the 40-plus-dollar price tag so steep? My advice is that. if you really need a keyboard,. you're better off finding a genuine third-party keyboard,. one with a stand. and a chin support. Those are way better than these half-baked copies. that are even worse than generic models. Now, taking this opportunity,. let's talk about cases I'd never normally consider buying. Ta-da! Here's a basket full. Once you slap one of these on,. your iPad turns into a kid's tablet. And here's how you. put the pen in. you have to poke it in horizontally like this,. then slide it back. Perfect for preventing kids from knocking it out. But seriously, which genius. came up with the idea of making a child-friendly case for the iPad Pro? Now, take a look at this one. This is even more outrageous. Look at this handle. it feels like it's going to snap off in my hand any second. And there's no stand either. You place it here,. and sure, it stands up,. but it feels like a gentle push would topple it over. Next up is this airbag case. The back of this thing is definitely top-notch for shock absorption. They've actually added these dashed lines here,. giving it a vibe like the airbags are stitched on. Let me show you another case. actually, it looks pretty cute,. kind of like a teenage girl's scrapbook style. It's got this slightly suede-like finish,. and there's a mesh layer at the bottom. But the mesh isn't taut at all; it looks kind of cheap and flimsy. And don't let its looks fool you. it's not cheap, either. It costs $19. Though I have to say, it's kinda practical. you can even use it to wipe your screen! Now, onto the next.

iPad case... Wait,. from Mac Studio. it's priced at $52,. but look. the magnet has already flown off! Time for some on-the-spot repairs. Don't. oh wow, that sound! Is there anyone out there. who can't stand the sound of metal scraping? Steer clear of this one, then. The scraping sound. is terrifying. Actually, despite its abstract design,. this thing is way sturdier than I expected. Let me show you how tough it is. you can't even bend it! And get this, there's even an aromatherapy tablet inside. But I'm not sure. what the point of that is. And then there's this weird cooling case. so modular,. and it costs $68. There's a MagSafe port at the back,. so you can attach a cooler for active cooling. But does the iPad Pro really need active cooling? Plus, this case is a nightmare. for carrying around—it's so easy to lose parts! Now, let's talk about this iPad case that costs just over a few bucks. Wow,. the craftsmanship is truly... uninspiring, to say the least. There's this huge hollowed-out section in the middle. I doubt. it uses much more material than a phone case. And as expected, when I put it on,. it's so thin that I seriously doubt it offers any real protection. By the way, when I was filming this video,. this case also arrived. The texture is truly... unbeatable. This super cool-looking case. costs $36. When I bought it,. the description said,. "Handmade, top-grain leather, genuine leather. ￥@#￥………… #@￥#￥. case." Honestly, it looks decent from the outside,. but the inside is kinda grimy. It feels like they just bought a generic case mold from somewhere. and slapped this leather on top. I'd say even $1 is too much. for that generic case part. I feel like I could tear it off. with a little effort.

The texture is just... unbeatable, as I said. So,. which one do you think offers better value? Actually, iPad cases these days are pretty decent overall. You generally get what you pay for,. and there aren't too many outrageous rip-offs. But there are a few special features you need to watch out for,. like the Apple Pencil slot. Some cases have this gap here,. and since the magnetic hold isn't super strong to begin with,. once you take it. out of your bag,. the pencil's basically guaranteed to fall out. At the very least, there should be a flap or a groove. to keep the pencil secure. Another thing is the charging port,. especially with thicker cases. You either need a larger opening. or a thinner cable. Trying to force a thick cable into a small opening. is just asking for trouble. Also, I'd recommend avoiding plastic soft cases. Tablets are often placed on various surfaces,. and these cases get oily easily. If they turn yellow over time,. it's like a triple whammy. of disappointment. Here's another interesting thing. you'll notice. there are several cases out there. that look almost identical. but have different prices. and slightly different promotional images. But chances are,. once you get them in hand, they're pretty much the same,. or even exactly the same. So, if you see similar cases,. just go for the cheaper one,. regardless of the brand. When it comes to choosing cases for Android tablets,. since their sales volume isn't as large. or consistent as iPads,. my advice is. to first check out the official cases to see if any catch your eye. If you like one, go for the official option. If not,. I've got a somewhat unconventional tip. search for iPad cases instead,. find a style you like. since those have usually been tested over time,. and then look for. a similar style for your Android tablet. Combine that with the tips I mentioned earlier about avoiding common pitfalls,. and you'll significantly reduce the chances of ending up with a dud. OK, so that's all for this video.

If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me. I'm Cherry. See you next time! Bye-bye.

=============

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qF3Bd3VnbnE

歡迎訂閱切狸~ 在這裡你會看到很多有趣的數碼資訊哦！ | Please don’t forget to subscribe 💋：https://bit.ly/3jQ3429

-------------------------------------------------- ----------------------------------

平板殼的花樣真的太多啦！

往期影片：

https://youtu.be/TnWxQ_6uo-Y

https://youtu.be/2rrFX_xR5BE

https://youtu.be/x8TaN2Mr4Nw

F O L L O W M E

Weibo: @大狸子切切里

Bilibili: https://space.bilibili.com/471303350

#數碼 #開箱 #保護殼 #平板 #ipad
item.content:encoded:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qF3Bd3VnbnE

值得閱讀的理由

  • 了解如何挑選適合自己的平板保護殼。
  • 認識不同保護殼類型的優缺點，尤其在支架、重量和材質方面。
  • 避免踩到購買平板保護殼的常見陷阱，省下冤枉錢。

摘要

開場：平板保護殼的兩難

作者開頭表達對於平板保護殼的複雜感受，認為它們不像手機殼單純，需要同時具備保護、支撐、增加功能甚至支援打字等多重用途，同時又不能太重，導致沒有完美方案。他提到無論價格高低，市面上許多保護殼令人失望。因此，他決定透過這部影片，幫助觀眾釐清支架、重量、材質與細節的重要性，避免重蹈他的覆轍。他強調這支影片絕非業配。

支架的重要性與基本類型

作者認為平板保護殼的支架功能並非選配，而是必需品。他用「沒有蓋子的泡麵」來比喻沒有支架的平板。不同的支架類型適合不同需求。他指出向後翻摺的保護殼通常實用、結構緊湊簡單，並支援低角度模式，特別適合繪畫或書寫，能改善眩光問題。他甚至認為一些第三方保護殼比官方的更好，例如會增加筆槽。作者也批評Apple昂貴的Smart Folio。

基本保護殼的選擇與陷阱

作者比較了兩款外觀幾乎相同的基本保護殼（價格分別低於10美元和約20美元），指出即使價格不同，外觀可能一樣。他特別警告喜歡用餐時使用平板的人，不要購買這種保護殼，因為其外部材質容易吸附桌面油污並沾染螢幕。此外，這類保護殼通常無法調整角度，觀看影片不便。對此，作者推薦向內翻摺的保護殼，這類保護殼較抗污且角度調整更靈活。或者，選擇結構簡單的「真支架」保護殼。

多功能與複雜支架設計

作者接著介紹了近期流行的「摺紙」式保護殼，這類保護殼宣稱功能比官方更多樣。他展示了一款能提供多種角度（低角度、直立/橫向）的保護殼，並實際示範了複雜的摺疊方式，指出某些模式相當繁瑣。他也展示了另一款帶磁吸背板的摺紙殼，它甚至可以分離並支援直立模式，作者雖然覺得「神奇」，但也表示越複雜的摺紙殼，展開後就越顯雜亂。作者提到團隊測試時都被其複雜性搞得一愣。作者也提到了市面上的旋轉式保護殼，但他並不喜歡，因為這類保護殼如果操作不當，容易發出刺耳的聲響。他建議如果真的需要直立模式，不如選擇磁吸式保護殼。

重量與保護性的權衡

作者接著探討保護殼的重量與保護性之間的權衡。保護性越差、結構越少，通常越輕。最輕薄的選項類似Apple官方的磁吸式保護蓋，具有簡單的兩段式支架，但邊緣暴露，保護性較差。全包覆式保護殼提供更好的保護，但會顯著增加重量（通常200-300克，帶加固背板的甚至超過400克）。作者觀察到，昂貴的保護殼往往笨重或設計不佳，似乎是為了堆砌規格而犧牲了使用體驗。若追求輕薄，建議選擇中階保護殼或Apple官方產品。

材質選擇與問題

在材質方面，作者表示平板保護殼的變化不如手機殼多。他提到了常見的幾種類型，以及一些較不傳統的材質。質感上，他認為親膚材質和人造皮革較佳，但親膚材質非常不耐髒，容易沾染油污，且邊緣可能出現剝落。

平價鍵盤保護殼的實測與批判

作者接著評測了市面上最重、功能最複雜的一類保護殼：藍牙鍵盤保護殼，這些產品常被定位為Apple Magic Keyboard（售價約330美元）的平價替代品。他指出這些幾十美元的鍵盤殼外觀確實相似，也支援自動配對和無線充電（指鍵盤本身）。然而，他強調Magic Keyboard的核心價值在於其整合的充電孔和能無縫切換到平板模式的順暢體驗。對於這些平價鍵盤殼，作者給予嚴厲批判。它們需要獨立充電（保留Type-C孔），展開時顯得雜亂。最關鍵的是，滑鼠存在明顯的延遲，導致難以精準點擊，體驗非常糟糕（比喻為「像開車一樣」需要提前煞車）。即使從支架上取下或闔上蓋子，藍牙仍會保持連接，導致螢幕鍵盤無法彈出。其中一款的觸控板中心區域難以按壓。此外，這些鍵盤殼非常笨重（超過600克，比iPad Pro本身還重）。作者認為以這種糟糕的使用體驗來說，幾十美元的價格仍然太高。他建議，如果真的需要鍵盤，不如選擇真正的第三方鍵盤搭配單獨的支架，這比這些「半成品」模仿品要好得多。

各種奇葩保護殼大賞

作者接著展示了一些他平常絕不會購買的「奇葩保護殼」。首先是一款將iPad變成「兒童平板」的保護殼，其筆槽設計奇特。接著是一款帶有把手的保護殼，把手感覺很不牢固，且沒有正規支架，只能靠不穩定的方式站立。還有一款「氣囊保護殼」，作者稱其背部的吸震能力一流，設計有模仿縫線的氣囊紋路。另一款外觀類似少女剪貼簿風格的保護殼，帶有麂皮質感和底部網格，作者認為網格部分廉價鬆垮，但設計可愛，售價19美元，意外地實用，甚至可以擦拭螢幕。一款設計類似Mac Studio的磁吸保護殼，售價52美元，但作者拿到時磁鐵脫落，使用時會發出令人不適的金屬刮擦聲。儘管外觀抽象，作者發現它意外地堅固，難以彎曲，內部還有一個不知用途的芳香片。一款模組化的「散熱保護殼」，售價68美元，背面有類似MagSafe的接口，可以外接散熱器。作者質疑iPad Pro是否真的需要主動散熱，並認為這款保護殼容易丟失零件，不便攜帶。最後是一款僅售幾美元的超薄保護殼，作者認為其做工差、中間大塊鏤空，實際裝上後非常薄，保護性令人存疑。

================================================

All along,. I've been really conflicted about tablet cases. For phones， no matter how fancy you make them,. it's all about materials and small details. At most, you add a leather case or a finger ring, right? But for tablets,. they expect the case. to protect,. to stand,. to add all kinds of mechanisms,. and ideally,. even to support typing. Oh, and. don't make it too heavy. So you can guess that. there's just no perfect solution. So,. these are the cases I bought this time. Oh my god, it's 2025 already,. but some cases still make me want to rip them off instantly. A 10-dollar case and a 20-dollar case. look exactly the same. And some cases that cost over 50 dollars. don't work well at all. So,. let me help you to figure out. how important the stand, weight, material. and some little details are. After this, you'll know exactly what to buy without regrets. Don't step into the same traps I did. This video is absolutely not sponsored. Let's get started. A tablet without a stand. is like instant noodles without a lid. I think a stand isn't optional. It's a must. But stands. aren't all the same. You need to pick based on your needs. Cases that fold backward like this. are usually useful. They have compact and simple structure,. and support low-angle mode. Especially if you draw or write,. hey,. laying it flat causes glare and color shifts,. and propping it up slightly fixes that. But honestly, some third-party cases. are better than the official ones. Same with magnetic attachment,. they also add a clasp. to secure the stylus. More importantly,. Apple's Smart Folio. costs about 80 dollars.

Might as well rob me. "Smart", my foot! But these third-party ones are also crazy. These two cases, for example. One costs under 10 dollars. and the other about 20 dollars. Can you spot any difference? Sounds like they're full of merits,. but for those who like to take their tablets out for meals,. heed my warning:. don't buy this kind of case. The outside is basically a rag. that soaks up all the grease from the table. and smears it on your screen. Plus, these cases usually don't adjust angles. and you have to move your head. So if you watch a lot of videos, get an inward-folding case. It's stain-resistant and more adjustable. This type of case. is also slightly sturdier. Or go for a real stand case,. but keep the design simple. Lately, a new type of folding case has become popular. It can be folded like origami. Look at this seller's show. It always has more functions than the official case. For example, this one. There are two low angles. And it raises for portrait or landscape mode. Let me show you how it works. As for the first mode,. fold it inward like this,. tuck it here,. and that's angle one. Flip it around. and it's angle two. The third mode. is quite tricky. First push outward,. and then it stands up. If you reverse this part,. that's another angle. But that's not the official way. Here is another one with a magnetic backplate. It's even crazier. First, lay it flat like this,. then flip it outward,. and it stands like this for use. After raising it,. it can actually detach. Then you can switch the angle. for portrait mode. Just amazing. Ignoring other factors,. I'd totally pick this one. But the more complex the origami case,. the messier it looks when unfolded.

Let me put it this way:. when I tested it with my team,. everyone froze for a second. There's also a rotating case on the market,. but I'm not a fan. Though it unfolds easily,. if not handled properly,. it creaks like crazy. If you really need portrait mode,. just get a magnetic case instead. But usually, different types. mean different weights. The worse the protection. and the fewer the structures,. the lighter it is. So the thinnest option. is the type like Apple's official magnetic folio. Tri-fold with two angles. It works for watching videos and writing. It's both a cover. and a stand. Exactly a simple work of art. But with exposed edges,. 'm still worried. Some of my teammates even said. they added edge film to use magnetic cases safely. Full-coverage cases offer better protection. but add weight,. usually 200 to 300g. You can try holding a box of milk with your iPad. to feel the weight. If it is the type with a reinforced backplate,. it would have to reach over 400 grams. It is equivalent to an 11-inch iPad Pro. And this type of case is super expensive. Unless you're really anxious about damage,. the experiebce of this kind is not the best. What's weird is that. pricey cases are usually bulky. or ugly,. like they're over-engineered for specs. to force "premium" vibes. So if you want a thin and light one, just choose the mid-range cases,. or Apple's own. As for materials,. tablet cases. aren't as flashy as phone cases. They are basically these types. Other materials also exist,. like cowhide case, portable protective case for children,. and velvet-mesh case. People with high EQ would say. it's a bit unconventional. For the texture,. soft-touch and faux leather win. But for stain resistance,.

soft-touch is awful. as it's easy to get smeared with grease. If it's pressed like this,. the edges might peel later. Anyone else's case peeling? Raise your hand and let me see. Take a look at these two. which have the most complex functions. and are the heaviest. The Magic Keyboard,. but. Bluetooth edition. But this is what I said. They'd never admit it. Everyone knows. Apple's Magic Keyboard is made of gold,. costing just about 330 dollars. All these cases claim to be its budget alternatives. They support auto-pairing,. even wireless and something else. And they are only around 40 dollars. So gerenous. But really? Well, yes. They can connect automatically. After all, it's Bluetooth. And they look similar to the Apple's, indeed. But the essence of the Magic Keyboard. is not just the keyboard. This port. can charge your iPad. And when you take it off,. it seamlessly transforms into tablet mode for continued use. The smooth feel. when opening and closing it. just makes perfect sense. Now, look at these others. they keep a Type-C port because the keyboard itself needs charging,. and when you open them up, it's just a mess. They're like new recruits trying to do yoga. without stretching first. Well, let's step back and say,. even if we just use it as a Bluetooth keyboard,. isn't it still decent? emmmmmmmmmmm. If you can tolerate the mouse delay, that is. iPad OS already has this exaggerated mouse acceleration,. and with the added delay,. you can never click exactly where you want. You have to brake in advance, just like driving a car. Even if you take it off the stand,. it's still connected. No matter how many times you click, the keyboard won't pop up. It's still connected even when you close the lid. And here's the kicker. one of them has a trackpad.

that supports full-area press,. but while it works around the edges,. pressing in the middle. is a struggle. After using it for a few minutes, I felt very frustrated. Is that it? Nope. Both keyboards weigh over 600 grams,. much heavier than the iPad Pro itself. At this point,. I suddenly thought,. why is the 40-plus-dollar price tag so steep? My advice is that. if you really need a keyboard,. you're better off finding a genuine third-party keyboard,. one with a stand. and a chin support. Those are way better than these half-baked copies. that are even worse than generic models. Now, taking this opportunity,. let's talk about cases I'd never normally consider buying. Ta-da! Here's a basket full. Once you slap one of these on,. your iPad turns into a kid's tablet. And here's how you. put the pen in. you have to poke it in horizontally like this,. then slide it back. Perfect for preventing kids from knocking it out. But seriously, which genius. came up with the idea of making a child-friendly case for the iPad Pro? Now, take a look at this one. This is even more outrageous. Look at this handle. it feels like it's going to snap off in my hand any second. And there's no stand either. You place it here,. and sure, it stands up,. but it feels like a gentle push would topple it over. Next up is this airbag case. The back of this thing is definitely top-notch for shock absorption. They've actually added these dashed lines here,. giving it a vibe like the airbags are stitched on. Let me show you another case. actually, it looks pretty cute,. kind of like a teenage girl's scrapbook style. It's got this slightly suede-like finish,. and there's a mesh layer at the bottom. But the mesh isn't taut at all; it looks kind of cheap and flimsy. And don't let its looks fool you. it's not cheap, either. It costs $19. Though I have to say, it's kinda practical. you can even use it to wipe your screen! Now, onto the next.

iPad case... Wait,. from Mac Studio. it's priced at $52,. but look. the magnet has already flown off! Time for some on-the-spot repairs. Don't. oh wow, that sound! Is there anyone out there. who can't stand the sound of metal scraping? Steer clear of this one, then. The scraping sound. is terrifying. Actually, despite its abstract design,. this thing is way sturdier than I expected. Let me show you how tough it is. you can't even bend it! And get this, there's even an aromatherapy tablet inside. But I'm not sure. what the point of that is. And then there's this weird cooling case. so modular,. and it costs $68. There's a MagSafe port at the back,. so you can attach a cooler for active cooling. But does the iPad Pro really need active cooling? Plus, this case is a nightmare. for carrying around—it's so easy to lose parts! Now, let's talk about this iPad case that costs just over a few bucks. Wow,. the craftsmanship is truly... uninspiring, to say the least. There's this huge hollowed-out section in the middle. I doubt. it uses much more material than a phone case. And as expected, when I put it on,. it's so thin that I seriously doubt it offers any real protection. By the way, when I was filming this video,. this case also arrived. The texture is truly... unbeatable. This super cool-looking case. costs $36. When I bought it,. the description said,. "Handmade, top-grain leather, genuine leather. ￥@#￥………… #@￥#￥. case." Honestly, it looks decent from the outside,. but the inside is kinda grimy. It feels like they just bought a generic case mold from somewhere. and slapped this leather on top. I'd say even $1 is too much. for that generic case part. I feel like I could tear it off. with a little effort.

The texture is just... unbeatable, as I said. So,. which one do you think offers better value? Actually, iPad cases these days are pretty decent overall. You generally get what you pay for,. and there aren't too many outrageous rip-offs. But there are a few special features you need to watch out for,. like the Apple Pencil slot. Some cases have this gap here,. and since the magnetic hold isn't super strong to begin with,. once you take it. out of your bag,. the pencil's basically guaranteed to fall out. At the very least, there should be a flap or a groove. to keep the pencil secure. Another thing is the charging port,. especially with thicker cases. You either need a larger opening. or a thinner cable. Trying to force a thick cable into a small opening. is just asking for trouble. Also, I'd recommend avoiding plastic soft cases. Tablets are often placed on various surfaces,. and these cases get oily easily. If they turn yellow over time,. it's like a triple whammy. of disappointment. Here's another interesting thing. you'll notice. there are several cases out there. that look almost identical. but have different prices. and slightly different promotional images. But chances are,. once you get them in hand, they're pretty much the same,. or even exactly the same. So, if you see similar cases,. just go for the cheaper one,. regardless of the brand. When it comes to choosing cases for Android tablets,. since their sales volume isn't as large. or consistent as iPads,. my advice is. to first check out the official cases to see if any catch your eye. If you like one, go for the official option. If not,. I've got a somewhat unconventional tip. search for iPad cases instead,. find a style you like. since those have usually been tested over time,. and then look for. a similar style for your Android tablet. Combine that with the tips I mentioned earlier about avoiding common pitfalls,. and you'll significantly reduce the chances of ending up with a dud. OK, so that's all for this video.

If you find this video interesting. Remember to hit that one-click Like, Subscribe & Bell Notification for me! This is very important to me. I'm Cherry. See you next time! Bye-bye.
